rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place stateful equations using initialized temporal recurrence
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators such as and, or, not(...), and implication =>
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place stateful equations using initialized temporal recurrence
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators such as and, or, not(...), and implication =>
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees inside annex agree only when the case supplies concrete bounds, named constants, or a visible relation
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the property
